message:
feat(robot): add --robot-interrupt for priority course correction

Implements programmatic agent interruption with intelligent state
detection, enabling AI orchestrators to stop and redirect agents:

Interrupt flow:
1. Capture previous state of each target pane (output, activity level)
2. Send Ctrl+C to interrupt running operations
3. Wait for agents to reach ready state (prompt visible)
4. Optionally send follow-up message to all interrupted agents

State detection:
- Polls pane output for idle prompts (claude>, codex>, shell prompts)
- Detects "waiting for input" text patterns
- Tracks agent type from pane title (claude, codex, gemini, user)
- Captures last meaningful output for context preservation

Options:
- --robot-interrupt=<session>: Target session name
- --interrupt-msg=<text>: Message to send after interrupt
- --interrupt-all: Include user panes (normally skipped)
- --interrupt-force: Send Ctrl+C even if agent appears idle
- --interrupt-no-wait: Don't wait for ready state
- --interrupt-timeout=<ms>: Timeout for ready detection (default 10s)
- --panes=<list>: Filter to specific pane indices

JSON output (InterruptOutput):
- session: Target session name
- interrupted_at/completed_at: Timestamps
- interrupted: List of pane keys that received Ctrl+C
- previous_states: Map of pane key to PaneState (state, last_output, agent_type)
- method: "ctrl_c" or "ctrl_c_then_send"
- message_sent: Whether follow-up message was delivered
- ready_for_input: Panes confirmed ready for new input
- failed: Any errors encountered
- timed_out: Whether ready detection timed out

Use cases:
- Stop runaway agents consuming resources
- Redirect agent focus to urgent tasks
- Coordinate multi-agent handoffs
- Emergency stops with preserved context

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
